response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which means he gathered 39 blueberries. Additionally David collected three times as many blueberries as Sophia. This implies that Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together Emily David and Sophia collected a total of 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. That totals to 76 blueberries altogether.

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. Each batch of muffins required 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make we divide the total number of blueberries by the number needed per batch. This gives us 76 divided by 9 which results in 8 full batches with some blueberries left over. Since they only sold full batches the leftover blueberries were not used.

***

Each batch of muffins was sold for $5. With 8 batches made they earned 8 multiplied by 5 which equals 40 dollars. The leftover blueberries could not be used for additional batches so they did not generate extra income. This means the total amount of money they made from selling the muffins was exactly 40 dollars.

***

The process of collecting and selling the blueberries involved several steps. First they gathered the blueberries then they made the muffins and finally they sold the batches. Each step required careful planning and execution. The money earned from the muffins was a result of their combined efforts. The youth who participated in this activity gained valuable experience.
